Some members of this genus have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Zosterophyllanthus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to contribute to their medicinal properties.
